hero-eyebrow: A planning and record tool for spine surgeons
hero-h1: Plan and record spinal instrumentation surgery
hero-tagline: Plan it. Scan it. Keep it.
hero-lead: Skeletal Plan helps spine surgeons plan spinal instrumentation, scan the implants used, and keep a structured operative record. It runs in your browser on any device.
hero-trustline: Free for clinical use · works offline · 22 languages · no account, no telemetry
montage-monitor-label: Theatre monitor · single or dual screen
how-h2: Plan it. Scan it. Keep it.
how-sub: From the pre-operative plan to a record you can use.
step1-p: Design the construct level by level: screws, rods, cages, osteotomies and correction forces, across normal or variant anatomy. It records the osteotomy and intended correction; correction angles come from dedicated deformity-planning software.
step2-p: Record the implants you used. Scan the barcodes to auto-fill the system, size, lot and expiry, or enter the sizes by hand. That is just as quick when screws come on a non-sterile tray with no packaging to scan.
step3-p: Keep a clean, exportable record that stays useful long after the case:
step3-li1: A PDF for the patient record and notes
step3-li2: An implant inventory for stock and cost
scan-caption: Real implant barcodes: GS1 DataMatrix, GS1-128 and a two-part HIBC (primary, then lot and expiry). Scan any with Skeletal Plan to see exactly what it is: the system, size, lot and expiry. Open the sample sheet for the full set of test codes.
dl-sub: It runs in any browser. That is the main way to use it on desktop and laptop. Phone apps and an offline file are also available.
dl-detect: Use it in your browser, or pick a build below.
dl-web-p: Any modern browser, on any device. Nothing to install, always the latest version, and it keeps working offline once loaded. Add it to your home screen or desktop to pin it like an app. This is the main way to use Skeletal Plan; the options below are alternatives.
dl-android-blurb: Native app (.apk). The full implant (GTIN) catalogue is bundled in the app, and barcode scanning is faster than the browser. Download on the phone and tap to install.
dl-android-fine: Preview: allow “install unknown apps” when asked.
dl-desktop-blurb: On a desktop or laptop, use Skeletal Plan in your browser. It's always up to date.
dl-ios-blurb: A native iOS build is fully functional and in internal testing with a small group. You can also use it in Safari today. Add to Home Screen to pin it as an app.
dl-offline-note: Only for a PC that can never reach the network, so it can't open the web app at all. A point-in-time offline single-file build can be copied onto it (e.g. by USB), and re-copied to update.
mfr-sub: Get your system scanning and sizing correctly in Skeletal Plan.
mfr-lead: The app matches scanned implants against an offline catalogue. To add or correct your system, send us both of the following. Each does a different job:
mfr-li1: Surgical Technique Guides (STG): so screws, rods and cages are sized correctly in the planner (diameters, lengths, ordering matrix).
mfr-li2: GTIN / UDI data: so the physical barcodes scan and resolve to the right implant (GS1 and HIBC).
mfr-both: Both are required: sizing needs the STG, scanning needs the GTIN.
mfr-card-blurb: Email your STGs and GTIN/UDI export and we'll add the system to the catalogue.
about-sub: Leeds Spinal Instrumentation Plan and Record · v3.51.00-beta
about-lead: A digital documentation tool for spinal surgery instrumentation. It records the pre-operative plan and the post-operative construct, replacing hand-drawn diagrams with a structured, exportable format. The web app is the main way to use it and runs in any modern browser on a connected hospital computer. Phone apps add quick implant scanning in theatre, and a single-file offline build covers the rare air-gapped machine that cannot load the web app even once.
hl-record-desc: Document the whole case in one place: every implant, plus the decompression, osteotomies, plates, rods and cross-links. A complete construct record that complements navigation and robotics.
hl-wholespine-desc: Pedicle screws, hooks, rods and fixation, occiput to pelvis.
hl-cages-desc: Interbody cages (ACDF to ALIF), cervical and lumbar disc replacement, Schwab osteotomies and corpectomy.
hl-deformity-desc: Anchor placement (screws, hooks, bands), planned correction forces.
hl-screws-desc: Choose the right trajectory for each level: pedicle, lateral mass, cortical (CBT), pars, translaminar and transarticular. The projected shank view uses published convergence angles for illustration.
hl-decompression-desc: Map the bony decompression itself by level and side using the Lee zones (central, lateral recess, foraminal, extraforaminal). The surgical detail that matters for research, registries and revision surgery.
hl-barcode-desc: Reads GS1 and HIBC, auto-filling system, size, lot and expiry, or enter the sizes by hand.
hl-traceability-desc: Per-component lot and expiry, with an auto-generated implant inventory.
hl-anatomy-desc: Transitional lumbosacral levels and non-standard thoracic and lumbar counts.
hl-export-desc: Export a PDF for the patient record. It also carries a link and QR that reload the whole plan back into the app, so the same PDF doubles as a visual record in the EPR and a research source. JPG and JSON export too.
hl-share-desc: QR codes and links share the plan with patient details removed (name, ID, dates, surgeon). Safe to send to theatre, admin or company reps to order the right implants.
hl-multilingual-desc: Over twenty languages, including right-to-left scripts (Arabic, Hebrew) and CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean).
hl-theatre-desc: Works offline, and private by default with no telemetry or accounts.
footer-text: Developed in Leeds, United Kingdom. Dual-licensed (Free Clinical Use / Commercial). Distribution via GitHub Releases.
